phase1.output: (Window appears with all controls: Label, Button, TextField, TextArea, CheckBox, RadioButtons, ComboBox, ListView, Slider, ProgressBar, etc.)
phase1.explanation:
JavaFX provides many built-in controls:

Label:
• Displays text (non-editable)
• Can be styled with CSS

Button:
• Triggers actions when clicked
• setOnAction() for event handling

TextField:
• Single-line text input
• getText() to retrieve value
• setPromptText() for placeholder

TextArea:
• Multi-line text input
• setPrefHeight() for size

CheckBox:
• On/off toggle
• isSelected() to check state

RadioButton:
• Used in ToggleGroup (only one selected)
• setToggleGroup() to group

ComboBox:
• Drop-down list
• getItems().addAll() to populate
• getValue() to get selected

ListView:
• List of items
• getItems().addAll() to populate
• getSelectionModel().getSelectedItem()

Slider:
• Numeric slider
• getValue() for current value

ProgressBar:
• Shows progress (0.0 to 1.0)
• setProgress(value)

Tooltip:
• Hover text
• setTooltip() to attach

Hyperlink:
• Clickable link
• setOnAction() for events

Separator:
• Divides sections

All controls can be styled with CSS!

phase3.prompt:
Write a JavaFX program that:
1. Creates a VBox with spacing 15 and padding 20
2. Adds a Label "User Registration"
3. Adds a TextField with prompt "Enter username"
4. Adds a TextField with prompt "Enter password" (use PasswordField)
5. Adds a CheckBox "Remember me"
6. Adds a ComboBox with "Student", "Teacher", "Staff"
7. Adds a Button "Register"
8. Shows the window
dialogueHints: PasswordField is like TextField but hides text
phase3.openingDialogue: Controls — the building blocks of your UI! 🧱
